word_list.pronunciation: ɡɛt ɔːɹ tˈeɪk ɔːɹ kˈiːp wˈʌnz mˈaɪnd ˈɔf ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to distract someone's attention or thoughts away from something, typically something stressful, worrisome, or unpleasant

examples.x: Going back to work helped take my mind off Ian’s death.
The good thing about running is that it takes my mind off any problems I have.
After a long day at work, she decided to take a bubble bath to get her mind off the stress and unwind.
Reading a captivating novel is a fantastic way to take your mind off reality and immerse yourself in a different world.
He played a game of tennis to get his mind off his worries and enjoy some physical activity.
Cassandra's love was the only thing that kept my mind off this condition of mine.

senses.definition: a state in which one is very deep in thought and not aware of one's surroundings

examples.x: During the lecture, I found myself in a brown study, thinking about unrelated matters instead of paying attention.
Sitting by the river, he went into a brown study, contemplating life's mysteries and his own future.
When she's in a brown study, it's challenging to get her attention; she becomes completely absorbed in her own world.
While waiting for the bus, he often slips into a brown study, imagining distant places and adventures.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ ɐ ðə wˈʌnsˈoʊvɚ

senses.definition: to examine a thing or person very quickly

examples.x: Can you give my paper a once-over?
Before heading to the job interview, I gave my resume a once-over to ensure there were no errors or missing information.
When considering buying a used car, it's essential to give it a once-over to look for any visible issues or signs of wear and tear.

word_list.word: if (it|this) (was|were) a snake, it would have bitten you
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf ɪt ɔːɹ ðɪs wʌz ɔːɹ wɜːɹ ɐ snˈeɪk ɪt wʊdhɐv bˈɪʔn̩ juː

senses.definition: said to mean that what a person is searching for is right where they are, but they have failed to notice it
used to tell someone that the zipper of their pants is open
used to say that a person is thinking about things that are unrealistic or impractical

examples.x: John is a dreamer with his head in the clouds.
Sam is always daydreaming and has his head in the clouds.
She's a talented artist, but she often has her head in the clouds, which can make it challenging for her to stay organized.
The professor's lectures are so abstract; it's like he has his head in the clouds and doesn't connect with the practical aspects of the subject.

senses.definition: not paying attention or not doing one's job properly, often resulting in mistakes or negative consequences

examples.x: Let's hope the regulators are not asleep at the wheel.
When the firemen arrived too late at the scene, the night watchman was accused of being asleep at the wheel.
I was asleep at the wheel during the meeting, I didn't take any notes.
The management team was asleep at the wheel when it came to addressing the safety concerns, they didn't take them seriously.
The government has been asleep at the wheel when it comes to addressing climate change, they haven't done enough to reduce carbon emissions.
